title: Paul Mescal's Supporting Actor Strategy for Hamnet
text: Paul Mescal will pursue a supporting actor Oscar nomination for his role as William Shakespeare in director Chloé Zhao's film Hamnet, rather than competing in the lead actor category. The decision by Mescal and distributor Focus Features comes down to screen time metrics. Analysis reveals that Mescal appears for approximately 44 minutes and 57 seconds, constituting 36% of runtime, while his costar Jessie Buckley appears for roughly 63 minutes and 49 seconds, or 51% of runtime. The article notes it is not a stretch to argue that Mescal's performance is in fact supporting Buckley's. This aligns with the film's narrative, as Agnes Shakespeare's frustration stems partly from William's extended absences in London. The supporting actor path also prevents vote-splitting, since Mescal is the clear lead in another awards contender, Mubi's The History of Sound. Hamnet premiered at Telluride Film Festival and won the People's Choice Award at Toronto International Film Festival.
